claim: Rotary embeddings outperform alibi positional encodings on length extrapolation beyond 4x training length
Scaling learning rate by sqrt(batch_size) reduces convergence time by 15-25 percent at 8B parameters
Grouped query attention reduces KV cache by 90 percent with less than 2pp accuracy drop at 7B scale
Layer pruning from 48 to 36 layers maintains performance when combined with knowledge distillation at 13B
Intermediate layer fusion (combining adjacent FFN outputs) reduces compute 8 percent with 1pp loss at 3B scale
SwiGLU activations outperform GELU by 0.5pp perplexity at sub-1B parameter counts
Token dropout at 10 percent rate during training improves robustness to corrupted inputs by 12 percent
Warmup duration of 1000 steps is suboptimal below 500M parameters; 200-400 steps preferred
Decaying embeddings with 0.9999 per-step factor reduces norm explosion in long sequences by 35 percent
Attention head dimensionality of 48 outperforms 64 in 8-head configs at sub-2B scale
Gradient clipping threshold of 0.5 prevents divergence better than 1.0 on synthetic reasoning tasks
Mixing experts (4-to-1 MoE ratio) improves token efficiency 15 percent versus dense feedforward at equal flops
Pre-layernorm consistently outperforms post-layernorm in stability across depths 12-80
LoRA rank of 8 saturates performance gains in 7B models; rank 16 shows diminishing returns
Entropy regularization on attention weights reduces overfitting to common tokens by 8 percent
Staggered KV projection (separate linear layers per head) recovers 0.7pp accuracy lost to compression
Cyclic annealing of learning rate with period 5000 steps accelerates convergence 20 percent vs fixed schedule
Initialization of attention biases to -infinity (instead of 0) improves first-layer learning speed 12 percent
Shared query-key projections in cross-attention reduce parameters 25 percent with under 1pp degradation
Flash attention with block size 128 outperforms block size 64 on sequence length generalization by 3 percent
Freezing embeddings after 50k steps prevents catastrophic forgetting in continual pretraining scenarios
Auxiliary loss weight of 0.01 on load balancing recovers 2pp accuracy in 16-expert sparse models
Depth-wise gradient scaling (layer-dependent clip per layer) reduces instability in 64-layer configs by 40 percent
Swapping attention order (key-query product first) reduces precision loss in low-precision arithmetic
Bias terms in feedforward layers can be pruned without loss below 7B model size
Exponential moving average weight decay with rate 0.999 outperforms L2 regularization on code benchmarks
Intermediate layer probing accuracy peaks at layers 40-50 percent depth; early layers are noise
Activation checkpointing of every third layer maintains 98 percent training speed while halving peak memory
Head-wise learning rates (scaled by head importance) improve convergence 18 percent in multi-head attention
Softmax temperature annealing from 1.5 to 1.0 over training stabilizes attention distributions by 25 percent
Sparse attention patterns (local + strided) reduce compute 30 percent with 0.3pp loss at 3B parameters
Contextual embeddings (position-dependent token embeddings) improve in-context learning by 7 percent at 13B
Layer normalization epsilon of 1e-6 is unnecessarily conservative; 1e-4 trains 12 percent faster safely
Causal masking with learnable mask sharpness parameter outperforms fixed masks by 1.2pp on autoregressive tasks
Tied embedding and output projections increase training instability but improve generalization by 0.8pp
Attention pattern variance across heads decreases with depth; average compression ratio gains 5 percent at layer 30+
Token frequency reweighting in loss (down-weight common tokens) improves rare token prediction by 15 percent
Cross-layer weight sharing (every 4 layers share weights) reduces parameters 80 percent with 2.5pp drop at 7B
Spectral normalization on query projections prevents attention collapse to single modes by 40 percent
Dynamic hidden dimension scaling (FFN width proportional to input norm) improves compute efficiency 22 percent
